LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS EUGENE CHIARELLI AND ARTER & HADDEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS DENIED BECAUSE THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHEN PLAINTIFF ACTUALLY SUSTAINED AN INJURY. THE SANCTION ORDER AND LETTERS COMPLAINING ABOUT FEES ARE TOO AMBIGUOUS TO ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF, AT THAT TIME, SUFFERED AN INJURY. JORDACHE ENTERPRISES V. BROBECK, PHELGER & HARRISON (1998) 18 CAL.4TH 739, 743. PLAINTIFF'S DISMISSAL OF THE CLAIMS AGAINST CHIARELLI DOES NOT BAR THE CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM RELATES ONLY TO THE PERIOD OF TIME WHEN HE WAS REPRESENTED BY DEFENDANT. AS TO THE BANKRUPTCY ASSIGNMENT, THE BANKRUPTCY COURT HAS EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF THIS ISSUE AND DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT IS A COLLATERAL ATTACK ON THE BANKRUPTCY COURT'S ORDER. THIS COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO NEGATE THAT ORDER. PLAINTIFF OBJECTIONS 1 AND 2 ARE OVERRULED; 3 IS SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO PARAGRAPH 5 OF THE BALIN DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED AND THE OBJECTION TO PARAGRAPH 7 IS SUSTAINED. MR. BALIN TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS EUGENE CHIARELLI AND ARTER & HADDEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: WILLIAM M. BALIN, ESQUIRE (BALIN & KOTLER, 415.252.8048) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ALLISON LANE COOPER, ESQUIRE (KRIEG KELLER SLOAN REILLEY & ROMAN, 415.249.8309) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS EUGENE CHIARELLI AND ARTER & HADDEN.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: Motion for summary judgment is denied based on the statute of limitations, is denied because there are triable issues of fact as to when plaintiff actually sustained an injury. The sanction order and letters complaining about fees are too ambiguous to establish that plaintiff, at that time, suffered an injury. Jordache Enterprises v. Brobeck, Phelger & Harrison (1998) 18 Cal.4th 739, 743. Plaintiff's dismissal of the claims against Chiarelli does not bar the claims against defendant. Plaintiff's claim relates only to the period of time when he was represented by defendant. As to the bankruptcy assignment, the Bankruptcy Court has exclusive jurisdiction of this issue and defendant's argument is a collateral attack on the Bankruptcy Court's order. This court lacks jurisdiction to negate that order. Plaintiff objections 1 and 2 are overruled; 3 is sustained. Defendant's objection to paragraph 5 of the Balin Declaration is sustained and the objection to paragraph 7 is sustained. MR. BALIN TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS EUGENE CHIARELLI AND ARTER & HADDEN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: WILLIAM M. BALIN, ESQUIRE (BALIN & KOTLER, 415.241.7360) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ALLISON LANE COOPER, ESQUIRE (KRIEG KELLER SLOAN REILLY & ROMAN, 415.249.8309) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING, SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS (FROM JANUARY 24, 2007) LEAVE TO AMEND. THE OBJECTION BASED ON THE CONTENTION THAT THERE WAS AN ASSIGNMENT IS OVERRULED. DEMURRERS, AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION, ARE OVERRULED BASED ON BEANE V PAULSEN. MS.
